steps.title: Interpret slope as the rate of change
steps.content: Slope measures $\frac{\Delta y}{\Delta x} = \frac{\text{change in total cost}}{\text{change in games}}$. This is the cost added for each additional game—the per-game price.

steps.title: Eliminate the y-intercept interpretations
steps.content: Choices B and C refer to the system's cost, which is a fixed initial expense. In $y = mx + b$, that fixed cost is the $y$-intercept (the value when $x = 0$), not the slope. The slope specifically measures the additional cost per game, not a one-time system fee.

steps.title: Read the slope from the graph and choose
steps.content: From the graph, the slope is 25. Therefore each game costs \$25. That matches choice A — A is correct.
